task: I’ve got a few home repairs and errands I want to keep straight, and I’m thinking of putting them into a simple note I can refer back to. What’s the cleanest way to lay that out so I don’t end up rechecking everything later?

turns.agent_response:
Use one simple note with **one line per item** and keep the fields in the same order every time.

### Clean layout
**Item | Due | What’s needed | Blocked by | Next step | Done?**

Example:

- Fix leaky faucet | Sat | plumber quote | call first | call 2 plumbers | no
- Pick up prescription | today | ID + card | none | go after lunch | no
- Replace smoke alarm battery | this week | battery | none | buy battery | no
- Call landlord about window | ASAP | photos | waiting on photo | take photo first | no

### Why this works
- **Due** keeps the urgent stuff at the top.
- **What’s needed** tells you what you’re actually dealing with.
- **Blocked by** shows what can’t move yet.
- **Next step** stops you from re-reading the whole thing later.
- **Done?** keeps finished items from mixing back in.

### Best way to use it
1. Write everything down once.
2. Sort by **due date first**.
3. Mark anything blocked so you don’t waste time on it.
4. Only keep the **next step**, not the whole plan in your head.
5. Cross it off or move it to a finished section when done.

If you want, I can turn that into a **very short note template** you can copy straight into your phone.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s fine. Simple is better. One line per thing, same fields every time — that’s the part that matters. I’d probably keep it even plainer:

- Item
- When
- What I need
- Next step
- Done / not done

No extra junk. If it gets too fancy, I won’t use it. The main thing is I can look once and know what’s waiting and what already got handled.
